titleStmt.title: Letter from Michael Cahill to the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, 29 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Michael Cahill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from solicitor Michael Frederick Cahill on the behalf of Dr Aimai Maneckji Cooper, a licentiate of the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI), regarding the removal of the name 'Grace' from Dr Cooper's certificates and records.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.persName: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
M. F. CAHILL,
Solicitor. 16, CLEVELAND ROAD,
ILFORD, ESSEX. 29. 5. 16 Dear Sir, Miss Aimai M. Cooper I lam much obliged by your courteous note to hand
on Saturday: and I enclose declaration of identity
with P. O. 21/. and am sending under separate
registered way (1) the Physicians Diploma and (2)
License in Midwifery each dated 3 February 1899. Kindly acknowledge safe receipt have the change of
name duly registered and return the diplomas to
me together with a letter from you (for the Medical
Register here) stating the change of name and
certifying that it has been duly made in your
books. Yours faithfully M. J. Cahill R. Phelps Esq
R.C.P.I.
I Michael Frederick Cahill of 16 Cleveland Road
Ilford Essex, Solicitor solemnly and sincerely declare
as follows: 1. I am well acquainted with Miss Aimai Maneckji
Cooper of 15th High Road Ilford. She was formerly
known as Aimai Grace Maneckji Cooper but has
now discontinued the name "Grace." 2. She is the same person as Aimai Grace
Maneckji Cooper mentioned in the Certificates
issued by the Royal College of Physicians in
Ireland on the 3rd day of February 1899 to
Aimai Grace Maneckji Cooper being the one a
License in Midwifery and the other a Certificate
that she is a Physician and Licentiate in
Medicine of the said College. She is also the
same person to whom a Qualifying Cerficate
in Medicine Surgery and midwifery was issued
on the 2nd February 1899 by the Conjoint Humanities
Board of the said Royal College of Physicians
and the Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland
and she is the person described in all the
said Certificates as Aimai Grace Maneckji
Cooper. M. J. Cahill Declared by the said Michael
Frederick Cahill at 61 Moorgate
Street in the City of London
this 29th day of May 1916
Before me
H. I Tiddlemen
A Commissioner for Oaths
